title: Nex Reference
text:
Contents
This reference documents the interpreter-level built-ins currently defined in src/nex/interpreter.cljc.

title: Nex Reference
text:
Built-in Functions
Foundational Classes
Scalar Types
Collection Types
Cursor Types
System Classes
Graphics Classes

title: Nex Reference
text:
Conventions

Method names are listed exactly as implemented.
Signatures are shown in Nex-style pseudocode.
nil means no useful return value.
Some behavior differs by runtime (jvm vs node), especially for File and Process.

title: Built-in Functions
text:
print
Writes all arguments to interpreter output as a space-separated line. Returns nil.

title: Built-in Functions
text: print(...args)

title: Built-in Functions
text:
println
println(...args)
Same output behavior as print in the current interpreter. Returns nil.

title: Built-in Functions
text:
type_of
type_of(value): String
Returns the runtime type name.

title: Built-in Functions
text:
type_is
type_is(type_name: String, value): Boolean
Checks runtime type compatibility (including built-in inheritance relationships like scalar types implementing Comparable/Hashable, and cursor types matching Cursor).

title: Built-in Functions
text:
Examples
print("hello", 42, true)
println("done")

let x := 12
print(type_of(x))                 -- "Integer"
print(type_is("Comparable", x))   -- true
print(type_is("String", x))       -- false

title: Foundational Classes
text:
Function
Built-in base class with deferred call-style methods.

title: Foundational Classes
text:
Cursor
Abstract iteration interface.



Method
Arguments
Returns
Description




start
none
Void
Reset iteration to the first position.


item
none
Any
Return current item.


next
none
Void
Advance to next position.


at_end
none
Boolean
Check whether iteration is complete.



Concrete runtime cursor types are documented in Cursor Types.

title: Foundational Classes

Method
Arguments
Returns
Description




compare
a: Any

Return negative/zero/positive ordering result.

title: Foundational Classes
text:
Hashable (deferred)



Method
Arguments
Returns
Description




hash
none
Integer
Return stable hash code for the value.

title: Foundational Classes
text:
Examples
let s := "nex"
print(s.compare("next"))          -- negative / 0 / positive
print(s.hash())

let n := 42
print(n.compare(10))               -- positive
print(n.hash())

title: Scalar Types
text:
String
Built-in scalar classes: String, Integer, Integer64, Real, Decimal, Boolean, Char.
All scalar classes are modeled as implementing Comparable and Hashable.

title: Scalar Types
text:
Integer
Bitwise operations use 32-bit integer semantics. Bit index 0 is the least-significant bit. For method calls on integer literals, wrap the literal in parentheses: (5).bitwise_left_shift(1).

title: Scalar Types
text:
Examples
let s := "  Nex  "
print(s.trim().to_upper())        -- "NEX"
print(s.contains("ex"))           -- true

let n: Integer := 7
print(n.plus(5))                  -- 12
print(n.pick())                   -- random integer in [0, 7)
print((5).bitwise_left_shift(1))  -- 10
print((6).bitwise_and(3))         -- 2

let r: Real := 3.6
print(r.round())                  -- 4

let b: Boolean := true
print(b.and(false))               -- false

title: Collection Types
text:
Array
This section covers the standard collection abstractions used throughout Nex code. Array, Map, and Set are built-in collection types. Stack[T] is a common generic collection class pattern built on top of Array[T].

title: Collection Types
text:
Stack[T]
Stack[T] is a generic last-in, first-out collection abstraction. It is not a built-in primitive type; it is typically defined as a class using Array[T] for storage.

Typical Definition
class Stack [T]
  create
    make() do
      items := []
    end

  feature
    items: Array[T]

    push(value: T) do
      items.add(value)
    end

    pop(): T do
      result := items.get(items.length - 1)
      items.remove(items.length - 1)
    end

    peek(): T do
      result := items.get(items.length - 1)
    end

    is_empty(): Boolean do
      result := items.is_empty
    end

    size(): Integer do
      result := items.length
    end
end


Methods



Method
Arguments
Returns
Description




push
value: T
Void
Push value onto the top of the stack.


pop
none
T
Remove and return the top element.


peek
none
T
Return the top element without removing it.


is_empty
none
Boolean
True when the stack has no elements.


size
none
Integer
Number of stored elements.





Notes

pop and peek usually require the stack to be non-empty.
A contract-based version should add preconditions for these operations.
Stack[T] is the canonical example of a user-defined generic collection in Nex.

title: Collection Types
text:
Examples
let xs: Array [Integer] := [10, 20]
xs.add(30)
xs.add_at(1, 15)                  -- [10, 15, 20, 30]
print(xs.get(2))                  -- 20
print(xs.index_of(30))            -- 3

let m: Map [String, String] := {"lang": "Nex"}
m.put("kind", "language")
print(m.get("lang"))              -- "Nex"
print(m.try_get("missing", "n/a")) -- "n/a"
print(m.contains_key("kind"))     -- true

let s1 := #{1, 2}
let s2 := #{2, 3}
print(s1.union(s2))               -- #{1, 2, 3}

let stack := create Stack[Integer].make
stack.push(10)
stack.push(20)
print(stack.peek)                 -- 20

title: Cursor Types
text:
ArrayCursor
Cursors implement the Cursor protocol: start, item, next, at_end.
Created by: arr.cursor()

title: Cursor Types
text:
Method
Arguments
Returns
Description




start
none
Void
Reset index to 0.


item
none
Any
Return current array element; throws at end.


next
none
Void
Advance index by one if not at end.


at_end
none
Boolean
True when index is beyond last element.

text:

Created by: str.cursor()

Returns
Description

title: Cursor Types
text:
MapCursor
Created by: map.cursor()









Method
Arguments
Returns
Description




start
none
Void
Refresh key snapshot and reset index.


item
none
Array[Any]
Return [key, value]; throws at end.


next
none
Void
Advance index by one if not at end.


at_end
none
Boolean
True when index is beyond key snapshot.

title: Cursor Types
text:
Examples
let xs := [1, 2, 3]
let c := xs.cursor()
from c.start() until c.at_end() do
  print(c.item())
  c.next()
end

let m := {"name": "Ada", "lang": "Nex"}
let mc := m.cursor()
from mc.start() until mc.at_end() do
  let pair := mc.item()
  print(pair.get(0) + ": " + pair.get(1))
  mc.next()
end

create Process

Returns

title: System Classes
text:
Examples
let con := create Console
con.print_line("Enter your name:")
let name := con.read_line()
con.print_line("Hello, " + name)

let f := create File.open("notes.txt")
f.write("line 1")
f.append("\nline 2")
print(f.exists())
print(f.lines())

let p := create Process
print(p.getenv("HOME"))
print(p.command_line())

title: Graphics Classes
text:
Examples
let w := create Window.with_title("Demo", 640, 360)
w.show()
w.bgcolor("white")
w.set_color("blue")
w.draw_rect(30, 30, 120, 80)
w.draw_text("Nex", 40, 70)
w.refresh()

let t := create Turtle.on_window(w)
t.color("red")
t.forward(80)
t.right(120)
t.forward(80)
t.right(120)
t.forward(80)

let img := create Image.from_file("sprite.png")
w.draw_image(img, 220, 100)
